Petition For Instructions To Trustee Aaron Chong; Compel Trustee To Provide Information Render Proper Accounting, And Distribute Trust Assets
Off Calendar Nov-22-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Yang, via ZOOM, for Aileen Chong-Jeung, Petitioner; Jerilyn Paik, via ZOOM, for Aaron Chong, Respondent; Jonathan Hoff, via ZOOM, for Alvin Chong, Objector.

The court points Ms. Paik to probate code 16461 and advises that the court reads the code as a classification of liability and not instructions. Ms. Paik provides oral advocacy for her position. Mr. Yang advises that his client is entitled to information and clarification regarding accountings. The court reminds Mr. Yang that there have been multiple accountings granted in this matter and asks why the Trustee should be obligated to account for future distributions. Mr. Yang states that he simply wants to confirm the assets of the trust. The court confirms with Ms. Paik that her client is ready to make equitable distributions of about $46,000. Mr. Yang then points to the amount of money spent litigating Alvin's petitions and does not believe that the distributions should not be equally shared, accordingly.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Instructions, etc., is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions To Trustee Aaron Chong; Compel Trustee To Provide Information Render Proper Accounting, And Distribute Trust Assets
Off Calendar Sep-21-2021 Continued To Nov-16-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Yang appeared by zoom for Aileen Chong-Jeung (Petitioner/Beneficiary).
Jerilyn Paik appeared by zoom for Aaron Chong (Trustee/Respondent).

The Court noted that there is no appearance by Alvin's counsel. Ms. Paik stated that she gave notice that they had 180-days from receipt of the accounting to make any objections and that it is permitted by Probate Code 16460. The court looked at the code, stated that it does not see anything regarding the 180-days, that it's a three-year statute of limitations and raised the issue of any problems with the sixth account being time barred. Mr. Yang stated that it is not time barred, that the sixth account does not carry any information regarding distribution, that there was no discussion and that the accounting is totally inadequate. The court stated that Mr. Yang needed to raise that in 2019. Mr. Yang stated that the at one-point Aileen was informed that there would be $34,000.00 available, that it was then reduced to $29,000.00 and that they need a form of accounting regarding the legal fees of Aileen. The court made it clear that it is not going to go back to the 6th account and stated that Mr. Yang has communicated with Ms. Paik about Aileen's share/the allocation of legal fees to Aileen. Discussion was held regarding the beneficiary's shares, distribution being made to make up for Alvin's 25 hours of legal fees, the reserve, the trust being distributed when there are not objections to the accounting, how the shares were calculated, etc. The court stated that the parties can incur additional fees, or they can talk. The Petition for Instructions to Trustee Aaron Chong, etc. is continued to November 16, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 (one last time for counsel to talk).

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jul-05-2016 Continued To Aug-02-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jerilyn Paik (via court call) for Aaron Chong. Alvin Chong in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that this is a petition to approve the terms of a settlement agreement and final distribution, that there does not seem to be an agreement, asked if the parties have talked further, that it cannot approve a settlement agreement that is not an agreement, that it does not know why the parties cannot come to an agreement, and asked Alvin Chong if he objects to distribution. Mr. Chong stated that he does not think so, that he thought they settled everything at trial, that he has not heard from his attorney in 6 months, that he was told to either settle now or lose everything, that Aaron has been in control since 2009, that there has not been a good accounting and asked why Aaron is the trustee. The Court responded that we are not here to re-try what happened at trial, that it does not know if it can grant final distribution and that it will give Mr. Chong time to put things in writing. Mr. Chong stated that he only objects to this continuing on and on. The Court responded that it is continuing because you and Aaron cannot agree and asked about turning over the key to the garage and the property manager. Mr. Chong responded that he thought that was all agreed upon. The Court stated that Mr. Chong did not sign off on the final settlement and asked if he agrees to the agreement. Mr. Chong stated that he was not aware of the signing and that his brothers agreement is unacceptable. The Court stated then there is no agreement and it cannot get done. Ms. Paik stated that Alvin did agree to the key to the common area and that the area of disagreement is the key to the garage. The Court stated that this is not an opportunity for a settlement conference and that it can continue the matter for the parties to work out an agreement or they can seek some other form of relief. Mr. Chong stated that the garage is not a common area. The Court stated that it does not know what the facts are and that it is not before it now, that it must be properly before the Court to be ruled on, that there is also a request to distribute cash to the 3 beneficiaries and asked if the parties want to proceed on that relief. Ms. Paik stated that they do not want to proceed with final distribution of the settlement agreement will be pending. The Court stated that it is not going to approve the settlement agreement, that they will need to amend the petition to resolve the other issues that are pending and that even if Mr. Chong objects the Court will still need an amended petition. Ms. Paik asked if Mr. Chong does not have objections and will consent to court approval can they move forward. The Court stated that it is not going to rule on a hypothetical. Ms. Paik stated that she is hoping if there is a continuance they will agree to the agreement or not. Mr. Chong asked if the court could make an order on time constraints so this does not go on longer. The Court stated that both brothers are dug in on this suggesting that something is there. The Court told Mr. Chong that he cannot represent himself without filing a substitution of attorney, that he needs to figure out the status with his attorney or find a new one. The Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement and for Final Distribution to House Trust and Beneficiaries was continued to August 2, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jerilyn Paik (via Court Call), for Petitioner Aaron Chong; David Yang for Eileen Chong (beneficiary).
Mr. Yang stated that he was present on behalf of Eileen Chong to object to the sale of the partial real property interest. Ms. Paik stated that Mr. Spaulding and her clients have settled all disputes between themselves, and that pursuant to their settlement agreement Mr. Spaulding has withdrawn his objections to the proposed sale going forward as requested by the petition before the Court. Ms. Paik responded that in accordance with the settlement agreement, Mr. Spaulding's petition to invalidate the sale and for partition of property has been withdrawn. Mr. Yang stated that his client's first objection to the approval of the sale is that the sale is below the market value of the property being sold, in that it is based on a valuation nearly one year old and not reflecting current market rates for real estate in San Francisco. He opined that the sale valuation of $1.32 million does not reflect the current value, which he estimated at $1.5 million. He stated that his client has not received a clear statement of the planned allocation of sale proceeds, and that there has been no breakdown of claimed legal expenses, which have reached $130,000 in one year. Ms. Paik responded that accounting issues are separate and to be addressed at a later time, but are not before the Court in this petition. She stated that once this partial interest is sold, the trust will have the funds needed to pay administrative expenses and then distribute the residue. She argued that the settlement was of all objections raised by Mr. Spaulding, but the settlement agreement does not address anything regarding Eileen Laub. The Court stated that he needed to review the settlement agreement. Mr. Yang again asserted that the petition should be denied as inequitable. Ms. Paik responded that the sale price represented 100% of the Probate Referee's valuation for the property interest. The Court stated that he needed to review the matter with staff. He informed Mr. Yang that any additional objections his client desired to raise must be submitted to the Court by August 17, 2015. Following that if the Court is satisfied that the matter is ready for submission, he will deem the matter submitted and the parties will be so notified by the Court. If further hearing is necessary, it shall go forward on August 24. The matter was continued to 2015 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.
